data.title: Guggenheim moves to the sidelines on Paramount Skydance after recent stock rally
data.description:
Analysts at Guggenheim downgraded Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) to "neutral" from a previous investment rating of "buy" on Friday, citing a significant stock rally since initiating coverage on the company earlier this month.

PSKY does not have a price target assigned to it at the moment. It previously had a PT of $13. Shares of the company have climbed 45.2% since the merger closed.

The research firm said its core view on the company is still unchanged, and they expect the new leadership to reinvigorate the content engine, improve technology solutions, and drive incremental efficiencies.

"We believe that the Skydance team thoroughly evaluated inputs—including the pace of cord-cutting pressure and realistic views of cost savings—in setting initial profitability goals on July 8, 2024, though we do expect 2026 profitability to be below that outlook given recent investments, including a multi-year rights agreement with the UFC," the research firm said in its note.

However, they believe that key strategic questions remain unanswered, particularly related to the balance of linear and direct-to-consumer economics as an increasing number of competitor DTC apps like ESPN and Fox One are made available to pay-TV customers with bundle subscriptions.

"Driving DTC growth without pressuring linear economics is an incremental risk to consolidated performance that we believe may be underappreciated by management," the research firm said.

data.title: Ubiquiti surges as Q4 results top estimates, boosts buyback program by $500M
data.description:
• Ubiquiti (NYSE:UI) shares surged 10% in premarket trading on Friday after its fiscal fourth-quarter results topped estimates by a wide margin.
  • The networking company said that for the fiscal year ended June 30, it earned an adjusted $3.54 per share as revenue surged 49.6% year-over-year to $759.2M. Ubiquiti said the growth came from its Enterprise Technology platform, and to a lesser extent, its Service Provider Technology platform.
  • In addition, Ubiquiti said its board of directors authorized a new share buyback program for up to $500M. The company also said it would pay a quarterly dividend of at least $0.80 per share in each quarter of fiscal 2026.

data.title: Ulta stands out within beauty category for tariff insulation, growth outlook -- Barclays
data.description:
As Ulta Beauty (NASDAQ:ULTA) heads into second quarter results next week, Barclays delivers an upgrade to the stock amid expectations that positive comparable store sales will gain momentum and the company’s relative insulation from tariff headwinds will translate into a negligible 0.2% unit cost increase.

Accordingly, the firm raised its rating on Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) to Overweight from Equal Weight and raised its price target by 14% to $589, representing 13% upside to Thursday’s closing price.

Since Kecia Steelman stepped into the role of chief executive, Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA) has undergone a series of “rapid and decisive changes” that focus on optimizing the point of retail distribution –which includes the non-renewal of the Ulta/Target partnership – improved promotional activity, and wide-range of price points and category offerings to offer an important distribution channel for both mass and prestige beauty brands.

As for the broader economic environment, Barclays analyst Andrienne Yih believes that beauty, in particular Ulta’s (ULTA) core customer (the beauty enthusiast), will “continue to over-index wallet share toward beauty, health, and wellness categories.”

“We believe that the overall beauty landscape, which has been normalizing since its peak in 2022, is in the process of stabilizing with an annual growth rate in the mid-single-digit range,” Yih said in her note to clients.

On the tariff front, Ulta (ULTA) has very limited tariff exposure with only ~1% of its merchandise directly imported last year, suggesting minimal direct-cost tariff risk.   

Yih shares her bullish outlook on Ulta (ULTA) with the majority of Wall Street analysts, while Seeking Alpha authors view the stock as a Hold. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating also gives Ulta (ULTA) a Hold based on its valuation and growth potential with a Quant score of 3.42 out of 5.

Ulta (ULTA) will report second quarter results after the market closes on August 28, expected to have earned a profit of $5.08 per share on $2.67B in revenue.

Ulta (ULTA) shares are nearly 2% higher in Friday’s premarket trading.

data.title: Parker-Hannifin extends share repurchase program to 20M shares
data.description:
Parker-Hannifin (NYSE:PH) said on Thursday that its stock repurchase authorization is now updated to buy back 20M shares.

Additionally, the company declared $1.80/share quarterly dividend, in line with the previous.

Shares were +0.54% Friday pre-market to $737.00.

data.title: Canada's retail sales drops 0.8% in July
data.description:
Retail Sales in Canada decreased 0.80 percent in July 2025 over the previous month, according to preliminary estimates.

Retail Sales in Canada increased 6.60 percent in June 2025 over the same month in the previous year.

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) and Tyson Foods (NYSE:TSN) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

For investors looking to gain further access to the dividend space, listed below are some popular dividend-focused ETFs that can be further analyzed:

data.title: Nuvini Group promotes Gustavo Usero to COO
data.description:
• Nuvini Group (NASDAQ:NVNI) said on Friday that Gustavo Usero has been promoted to Chief Operating Officer, effective September 1st, 2025.
  • Usero currently serves as Group Operating Director at Nuvini, where he leads operational excellence, financial performance, and strategic execution across the group’s portfolio. 
  • In his new role as COO, Usero will continue these efforts while leading the expansion of operations across Latin America.

data.title: Netgear initiated with bullish views at Stifel
data.description:
Stifel began coverage of connectivity solutions provider Netgear (NASDAQ:NTGR) with a Buy rating.

Shares of Netgear rose about 1% premarket on Friday.

Analysts led by Tore Svanberg highlighted three key investment points.

Firstly, the analysts said that the company has a differentiated ProAV platform. Netgear's Business segment (NFB) is becoming the primary growth driver, driven by differentiated ProAV (AV-over-IP) platform, expanded broadcasting and channel partnerships, and broadened solution portfolio that now integrates networking and security through a recent Exium acquisition. It is positioned for double-digit revenue growth and margin expansion in the near-to-medium-term, creating achievable pathway for Netgear to return to profitability in 2027, according to the analysts.

Secondly, there are home networking growth opportunities. The analysts noted that the segment is undergoing a product and strategic pivot designed to stabilize revenue, expand gross margin, and position the company as a trusted, U.S.-based alternative. The analysts expect the Home Networking segment to remain fairly steady in the coming quarters, potentially delivering slight year-over-year growth in 2026.

Lastly, Svanberg and his team highlighted enhanced subscription services. "Perhaps the most structurally transformative element of NETGEAR’s strategy lies in the scaling of its subscription services; specifically, (i) Insight for SMB network management; and (ii) Armor for consumer cybersecurity," said the analysts.

The analysts added that subscription revenue has consistently shown quarter-over-quarter growth, with Insight (cloud -based network management platform) offering centralized configuration, monitoring, and security for Managed Service Providers, or MSPs and small and medium-sized businesses, or SMBs.

Svanberg and his team noted that Armor provides router-level protection for up to 50 devices per household, VPN, ad-blocking, and extended warranty features, creating multiple layers of value within a single subscription.

data.title: AirNet Technology announces $180M registered direct offering
data.description:
• AirNet Technology (NASDAQ:ANTE) said on Friday that it has entered into a definitive agreement with investors for the purchase and sale of 80.8M of its ordinary shares, par value US$0.04 each, and accompanying warrants at a combined purchase price of $2.227 per ordinary share and accompanying warrant in a registered direct offering. 
  • The warrants are immediately exercisable at an exercise price of $3.3405 per ordinary share and will expire five years from the date of issuance. 
  • The gross proceeds to AirNet from the offering are expected to be approximately $180M, before deducting offering expenses payable by the company. 
  • AirNet intends to use the net proceeds from the proposed offering for general corporate and working capital purposes.

data.description:
